name: Leveraged Rebalance
overfit_notes: Single tuned allocation (60%) with no signal sensitivity — annual rebalance is mechanical. 60% is a deliberate choice (vs 50%, 70%, or 100%) but the strategy has no other parameters to tune.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: 60% TQQQ at all times

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: No exit; annual rebalance back to 60%

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Execution: TQQQ

name: IBS ATR Stop
overfit_notes: IBS thresholds 0.1/0.9 are extreme (top/bottom 10% of daily range) — canonical. ATR 2x chosen; 3x also tested and viable. Tested: IBS exits 0.7 (45%/-51%), 0.8 (49%/-50%), 0.9 (46%/-43%) all viable; entry 0.1 vs 0.2 differ ~5pp CAGR. Mild sensitivity, no sharp peaks.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: TQQQ IBS = (close − low)/(high − low) < 0.1 → equal-weight 1/3 TQQQ + 1/3 SOXL + 1/3 TECL

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: TQQQ IBS > 0.9 OR TQQQ close < entry − 2 × ATR(14, Wilder)

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal & ATR: TQQQ. Execution: TQQQ / SOXL / TECL

name: RSI Three Vote
overfit_notes: Graduated entry at 3 RSI thresholds [20,25,30] — each is canonical oversold level. Tested individually: smooth gradient across 15-35 (no spike). Vote structure naturally robust to single-threshold misfits.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Count n = # of RSI(2, Wilder) thresholds breached: <20, <25, <30. Basket weight = n/3 (weighted)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: RSI(2) ≥ 30: liquidate basket

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / SOXL / TECL (equal-weight)

name: Range Breakout
overfit_notes: ATR multiplier swept 1x-5x: 3x is local peak but 4x/5x within ~6% Sharpe (plateau). Fixed % stops (5-15%) all dramatically worse than ATR. ADX 25 / SMA 200 are canonical. Multiple tunables but each sits on stable region.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: All three must hold: QQQ > SMA(200), yesterday's QQQ range > the day-before's, ADX(10) > 25 → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: TQQQ ≥ 20-day high OR TQQQ < (price − 3 × ATR(14, Wilder), ratcheting up) OR QQQ < SMA(200)

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ

name: SMA200 RSI Tiers
overfit_notes: RSI overbought threshold sweep: >60 (24%/0.63), >70 (29%/0.70), >80 (31%/0.74) — mild gradient, not a sharp peak. RSI<30 dip and SMA200 are canonical. Multiple thresholds tuned but within reasonable ranges.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: TQQQ > SMA(200): 50% on first entry from cash, 100% on RSI(2, Wilder) < 30 dip, 20% on RSI(14, Wilder) > 70 overbought, else hold current weight (sticky)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: TQQQ ≤ SMA(200): cash

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal & Execution: TQQQ

name: SMA200 Pyramid
overfit_notes: Pyramid increment swept 10%/15%/20%: all ~30%/-50%/0.72-0.74 CAGR (plateau). SMA200 trend filter is canonical. The 5% gain step and 15% size add tuned but robust to nearby values.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: QQQ > SMA(200): start at 50% TQQQ; +15% per 5% TQQQ gain above entry (cap 100%)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: QQQ ≤ SMA(200): cash; reset entry/weight

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ

name: SMA Five Vote (150 weighted)
overfit_notes: SMA-vote with SMA(150) weighted 5× (since it tested best as a standalone). Other periods (20/50/100/200) still contribute 1× each. Bias toward SMA(150) is explicit — overfit risk inherited from the standalone SMA150 (which was moved to potentials). The other periods provide diversification.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Count n = # of SMA periods exceeded by QQQ across [20, 50, 100, 150×4, 200] (8 entries, SMA(150) appears 4×). TQQQ weight = n/8 (weighted)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Position scales down each time an SMA is lost

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ

name: Donchian Five Vote
overfit_notes: 4-Donchian midline vote across [50,100,150,200]. Individual results: D50 (38%), D100 (31%), D150 (18%/-65% — bad outlier), D200 (37%). Vote (32%) hides D150 weakness via diversification. The 4 canonical periods are reasonable but D150 inclusion is questionable.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Count n = # of Donchian midlines (50, 100, 150, 200, 250) exceeded by QQQ. TQQQ weight = n/5 (weighted). Midline = (period high + period low) / 2

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Position scales down each time a midline is lost

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ

name: Momentum Vote
overfit_notes: HIGHEST overfit risk in ensemble. Three signals (ROC, UpDay, TII) ALL use n=20 lookback, and ALL three peak sharply at exactly n=20: ROC Sharpe 0.83 (n=20) vs 0.50 (n=15/25); UpDay 0.79 vs 0.39/0.61; TII 0.71 vs 0.42/0.53. Three correlated signals overfit to same window. Vote helps but doesn't escape n=20 dependency. Out-of-sample edge probably much smaller.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: Count n = # of bullish signals: ROC(20)>0, UpDay(20)>10, TII(20)>10. TQQQ weight = n/3 (weighted)

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Position scales down each time a signal flips bearish

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ

name: Trend Stretch Exit
overfit_notes: Stretch exit at 20% tested across 15/20/25/30/35/40 (binary): plateau around 15-25% (CAGR 33-37%, Sharpe 0.77-0.91). Graduated variants all underperform binary. Entry at <5% stretch and SMA200 are reasonable. The 20% exit is in a stable region.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: QQQ > SMA(200) AND stretch = (price − SMA) / SMA < 5% → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: QQQ < SMA(200) OR stretch > 20%

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ

name: Golden Cross ATR
overfit_notes: EMA(50)>EMA(200) is the classic golden cross — canonical. ATR multiplier swept 1x-5x: 3x is local peak with broad shoulder (4x/5x within 7%). Fixed % stops dramatically worse — ATR is the right tool. Few tunables, mostly on stable regions.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: EMA(50) of QQQ > EMA(200) of QQQ → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: TQQQ < (price − 3 × ATR(14, Wilder), ratcheting up) OR EMA(50) ≤ EMA(200)

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. ATR & execution: TQQQ

name: Range Compressed
overfit_notes: Compression multiplier 1.1 swept 0.9-1.3: gradient is SMOOTH (no spike). Plateau at 1.05-1.20 (Sharpe 0.78-0.82). 200d median + 25/200d ranges are reasonable lookbacks. The least overfit strategy in the ensemble — classic robust signal shape.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: QQQ > 200-day median AND 25-day avg range < 110% of 200-day avg range → 100% TQQQ; only one true → 50%

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: Neither true → cash

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ

name: MFI14 Hyst
overfit_notes: MFI(14) at 40/60 hysteresis. Entry swept 40-80: peak at >55 (Sharpe 0.81), neighbors smooth (>50: 0.66, >60: 0.77, >65: 0.73). Exit swept 20-40 with entry at 55: peak >55/<35 (0.85). 40/60 sits in stable region; >55/<35 might be even better. MFI itself is volume-weighted RSI — canonical indicator.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: QQQ MFI(14) > 60 → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Exit
rules.text: QQQ MFI(14) < 40 → cash

rules.label: Hold
rules.text: 40 ≤ MFI(14) ≤ 60 → hold current position (hysteresis)

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ

name: Vol Regime 20
overfit_notes: Realized vol thresholds (20%/30%) on QQQ. Sensitivity sweep across (LOW, HIGH) pairs from 12/20 to 20/32 showed smooth Sharpe progression (0.53 → 0.82, no cliffs). Neighbors within ±5% Sharpe. Concept (vol-targeting) is mainstream. The 0.5 middle-tier weight is a tuned parameter. The 20/30 selection was discovered via iterative testing — caveat that more search increases overfit risk.

rules.label: Compute
rules.text: 20-day annualized realized vol of QQQ returns

rules.label: Allocation
rules.text: vol < 20%: 100% TQQQ; 20% ≤ vol < 30%: 50% TQQQ; vol ≥ 30%: cash

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ

rules.label: Components
rules.text: LeveragedRebalance, IBSATRStop, RSIThreeVote, RangeBreakout, SMA200RSITiers, SMA200Pyramid, SMAFiveVote (150×4), DonchianFiveVote, MomentumVote, TrendStretchExit, GoldenCrossATR, RangeCompressed, MFI14Hyst, VolRegime20 (14 sub-algos) + CashReserve (~3% sleeve). All 4 vote-based sub-algos (RSI3, SMA5, D5, Mom) use pure weighted rule: position = n/N.

rules.label: Weighting
rules.text: Each sub-algo gets a weight; equity reset to weight-proportional shares annually.
